title: Audrey Bergner at the Sticker-Covered Tierra del Fuego Border Sign in Southern Argentina
description: San Sebastián, Tierra del Fuego: Audrey Bergner pauses to celebrate a milestone at the weather-beaten border sign marking the entrance to Argentina’s southernmost province. The sign, densely layered with colorful stickers from intrepid travelers across the globe, stands as a vibrant mosaic against the stark, windswept Patagonian landscape under a brilliant cobalt sky. Soft, natural light illuminates the scene, catching the metallic texture of the signpost and the traveler’s enthusiastic expression as she interacts with the landmark. In the background, the vast, arid terrain stretches toward the horizon, punctuated by a solitary white transport vehicle and the distant, rolling silhouettes of the border plains. The crisp depth of field keeps both the subject and the intricate details of the stickers in focus, capturing the raw, adventurous spirit of overland transit between Chile and Argentina. Such frontier crossings represent the quintessential experience of traversing the rugged isolation of the Austral region, bridging the gap between nations at the end of the world. This moment encapsulates the tangible history left behind by those who brave the long, windswept roads leading into the heart of the southern wilderness. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
